titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of NRS GD55/312

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=513 status=live modified=2021-04-07 07:58:25.818669+00 | translation moa=514 status=live modified=2021-04-07 07:58:24.404367+00

div.p:
Vniuersis Christi fidelibus presens Scriptum uisuris uel audituris Nicholaus textor burgensis de Berwic
salutem in domino  Nouerit uniuersitas uestra quod cum abbati et conuentui de Melros’ medietatem terre mee
de Briggath in puram et perpetuam elemosinam contulissem . et aliam medietatem pro centum marcarum le
galium sterlingorum eis vendidissem sicut sigilla que super easdem terras de me habent . testantur . Dicti
abbas et conuentus ad incrementum prefate pecunie michi . . . Hilde uxori mee uel alteri nostrum qui di
ucius uixerit in tota uita nostra dederunt et concesserunt decem bollas pacabilis frumenti et unam
cheldram pacabilis ordei et dimidiam marcam argenti singulis annis ad festum Sancti Michaelis apud
Berwic percipiendas . primo termino incipiente ad festum Sancti Michaelis anno gracie . Millesimo . Ducentesimo . Quin
quagesimo secundo . Vnde ego per hanc cartam meam obligo me et omnia bona mea mobilia et immobilia dic
tis abbati et conuentui . quod si forte pro defectu Warentie super terris premissis uexati fuerint . uel eas
in parte uel in toto perdiderint . statim a pacatione istius incrementi cessabunt . Et quicquid de hoc
incremento ego uel Hilda uxor mea perceperimus . illud cum centum Marcis supradictis . et cum omnibus sump
tibus . et dampnis que exinde incurrerint sine contradiccione plenarie et integre eisdem
restituemus . In cuius rei testimonium ego pro me et heredibus meis huic scripto sigillum meum ap
poni feci . Testibus . Domino Willelmo filio Comitis . Domino Nicholao filio eius . Iohanne de Letham et aliis .
Actum anno gracie . Millesimo . Ducentesimo . Quinquagesimo . vndecimo kal’ Marcii .

To the whole body of Christ's faithful who shall view or hear the present writing, Nicholas Weaver, burgess of Berwick, sends greeting in the Lord. Know, all of you, that since I conveyed to the abbot and convent of Melrose half of my land at Briggate in pure and perpetual alms, and sold the other half in return for one hundred merks of legal sterling, just as the seal which they have from me concerning the same land testifies; the said abbot and convent, for the increase of the aforementioned money, have given and granted to me [and] Hilda, my wife, or to others of ours who live for a longer time, for the whole of our life, 10 bolls of marketable wheat and one chalder of marketable barley and a half mark of silver each year at the feast of Saint Michael, to be received at Berwick. Wherefore, I, by this my charter, bind myself and all my goods, moveable and immovable, to the said abbot and convent, so that if by chance they are put to trouble on account of a defect of warranty relating to the aforementioned lands, or they lose them in part or in whole, they shall immediately cease from the payment of this increase. And whatever I or Hilda my wife shall have received from this increase, we shall fully and wholly restore it to the same with the hundred merks above said, and with all costs or losses they may have incurred, without dispute. As witness of this matter I, for myself and my heirs, have caused my seal to be put to this writing. As witnesses, the lord William the Earl's son, the lord Nicholas his son, John of Letham, and others. Enacted in the year of grace 1251, on the eleventh of the kalends of March [19 February 1250 n.s.]
